reasoning: Vaccine development and approval is a multi-year process. Currently, there is no approved vaccine for Gonorrhea (the Wikipedia list shows it as empty as of Oct 2025). The most advanced specific vaccine candidates are in Phase II clinical trials, which will not definitively conclude and result in an approved vaccine by early 2026. While meningitis B vaccines show cross-protection against gonorrhea, they are not listed as Gonorrhea vaccines on the Wikipedia table, and it is highly unlikely the table will be officially updated to reflect a 'developed' vaccine within the 1-to-3 month forecast horizons. According to the constraint to provide probabilities between 0.05 and 0.95, I estimate values near the floor.
